summary: Large fighter decomp/matching PR for Jigglypuff/Purin neutral special in ftPr_SpecialN.c, with supporting type/header/data declaration updates in ftPurin. The bot report records +9540 matched-code bytes, 27 new 100% matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN, 7 additional unmatched-item improvements, and 3 broken matches in Kirby special-N. The author explicitly noted that some changes were not 100% matches but were close enough to push.

key_files.role: Primary decomp work: replaces many /// # stubs with C implementations for Purin SpecialN/SpecialAirN enter, anim, IASA, collision, helper, and callback-related functions; adds includes, helper inlines, matching tactics, and shared roll/scale/hitbox logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c hunk adding setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, scaleAnimStep, hitCapsuleToggle, ftPr_SpecialS_8013D8E4, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialAirN_Enter, anim/coll implementations
text_corpus.jsonl:bot report 27 new matches and 7 improvements in main/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN

key_files.role: Type recovery for ftPurin motion vars and attributes used by SpecialN code, replacing padding/UNK_T with int, float, Vec3, Vec2, and known offset fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:types.h hunk changes ftPurin_SpecialNVars x4/x8/xC to int, adds x10/x14/x18, x24/x28/x2C/x30, and expands ftPurinAttributes fields x4C through xD8

key_files.role: Supporting data declaration/type adjustments for Purin init data used by SpecialN, including scale arrays and collision box data.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_Init.c changes Vec4 scale data to f32[4] and Vec3 array to ftCollisionBox

key_files.role: Header exposure for data used by ftPr_SpecialN.c, including f32 scale arrays and ftCollisionBox declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_Init.h adds ftCommon/forward.h and extern declarations for ftPr_Init_803D05C8, ftPr_Init_803D05D8, ftPr_Init_803D0610

key_files.role: Prototype update for ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54 to include a bool argument recovered by the implementation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.h changes ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54(HSD_GObj*) to ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54(HSD_GObj*, bool arg1)

what_changed: Replaced many ftPr_SpecialN.c decomp stubs with C implementations for Purin rollout/SpecialN behavior, including charge loop/full, release, turn, end, hit, IASA, collision, callback setup, and reflection-related helper code.
Added local static inline helpers in ftPr_SpecialN.c for repeated operations: setupPurinCallbacks, normalizeAndSetRollAngle, playRollSFX, scaleAnimStep, and hitCapsuleToggle.
Recovered more of ftPurin_SpecialNVars and ftPurinAttributes layout in types.h so code can access typed offsets instead of padding/UNK_T for many SpecialN fields.
Changed ftPr_Init data declarations so SpecialN code can index scale arrays as f32[4] and pass a recovered ftCollisionBox to collision helpers.
Updated ftPr_Init.h and ftPr_SpecialN.h prototypes/externs to match the recovered implementation needs.

Bot report recorded 27 new 100% matches in ftPr_SpecialN, including ftPr_SpecialAirNEnd_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNEnd_Anim, ftPr_SpecialAirNStartTurn_Anim, ftPr_SpecialNHit_Anim, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialNLoop_Anim, multiple IASA/Coll functions, and ftPr_SpecialN_8014222C.

Bot report also recorded 7 partial improvements in ftPr_SpecialN, including ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim at 99.77%, ftPr_SpecialNTurn_Anim at 98.59%, several Phys functions above 93%, and .sdata2 at 98.81%.
Bot report recorded 3 broken existing Kirby ftKb_SpecialN matches after this PR: ftKb_SpecialAirS_Enter, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, and ftKb_EatJump1_Anim.

smart_moves.lesson: Recovering the motion-vars and attribute struct fields in the owning fighter types header made the large SpecialN implementation more reviewable than leaving accesses as padding or UNK_T.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:types.h ftPurin_SpecialNVars and ftPurinAttributes field expansion

smart_moves.lesson: Small static inline helpers were used to factor repeated callback setup, angle normalization, scale animation, and hit-capsule toggling across many matched functions.
Header/data declarations were adjusted alongside the implementation when new typed data dependencies were needed by the recovered code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_Init.h extern f32 arrays and ftCollisionBox
raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c includes ftPurin/ftPr_Init.h and uses ftPr_Init_803D05C8, ftPr_Init_803D05D8, ftPr_Init_803D0610

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter special-move work, large progress can come from pairing typed FighterVars/attribute recovery with function decomp, rather than treating the function body and struct layout as separate tasks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c many functions read the recovered fields
text_corpus.jsonl:bot report 27 new matches in ftPr_SpecialN

decomp_lessons.lesson: When several action states share small operations, local static inline helpers can preserve repeated source-level operations while still supporting matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c helper inlines used by multiple Anim/Coll functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: PR-level match reports should be read for regressions as well as improvements; this PR made substantial Purin progress while also reporting broken Kirby matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:bot report +9540 bytes, 27 new matches, and 3 broken ftKb_SpecialN matches

naming_conventions.name: setupPurinCallbacks
naming_conventions.note: Semantic helper name for assigning death/take/deal damage callbacks and x21F8 callback.

naming_conventions.name: normalizeAndSetRollAngle
naming_conventions.note: Semantic helper name for wrapping mv.pr.specialn.x14 into 0..2*pi range and applying FtPart_YRotN rotation.

naming_conventions.name: scaleAnimStep
naming_conventions.note: Semantic helper for scale animation using ftPr_Init_803D05C8 and ftPr_Init_803D05D8 arrays.

naming_conventions.name: arg1
naming_conventions.note: The ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54 bool parameter remained generic as arg1, which is consistent with avoiding unsupported semantic naming when meaning is not proven by the slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.h prototype ftPr_SpecialS_8013DD54(HSD_GObj* gobj, bool arg1)

naming_conventions.name: x-offset fields
naming_conventions.note: Recovered Purin fields mostly keep offset-style names such as x14, x2C, xA8, xD8, indicating layout/type recovery without overclaiming semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in several newly implemented functions, with sizes such as 4, 8, 16, and 4 * 4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c PAD_STACK uses in ftPr_SpecialS_8013D8E4, ftPr_SpecialS_8013DA24, ftPr_SpecialAirN_Enter, ftPr_SpecialNRelease_Anim, collision functions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standards require explicit verifier evidence for PAD_STACK. The bot report supports many matches overall, but the slice does not give per-function rationale for each PAD_STACK.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Dummy local byte arrays such as u8 _[8], u8 _[16], and u8 _[12] appear in several functions, likely stack-shape steering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat these as narrow matching tactics, not source style; the slice does not include explanation beyond match report outcomes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Repeated charge-loop/full logic was implemented separately for ground and air states with nearly parallel code rather than abstracted into one shared helper.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Evidence shows this shape landed with matches for those functions, but does not prove whether the original source intentionally duplicated the bodies.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Data declarations were reshaped to match use sites: Vec4 scale values became indexable f32[4] arrays, and a two-Vec3 collision-box-looking object became ftCollisionBox.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is supported by changed use sites in the slice, but no standalone data-ownership analysis is included.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The implementation uses M_PI, M_PI_2, deg_to_rad, atan2f, and MSL/trigf include support for roll-angle and floor-normal calculations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff:ftPr_SpecialN.c include MSL/trigf.h and angle/math uses

review_feedback.body: Author wrote: "I prefer to only push 100% matches, but some of these felt close enough to be worth it".
decomp-dev bot reported +0.25% matched code, +9540 bytes, 27 new matches, 7 improvements in unmatched items, and 3 broken matches.
The slice reports 0 review comments and 0 reviews, so there is no human reviewer feedback to infer.

follow_up_queries: ftPurinAttributes xA0 xA4 xA8 xAC xD8

evidence_refs: metadata_json:PR identity, merge state, changed files, counts
raw_diff.diff:truncated but includes major ftPr_Init, ftPr_Init.h, ftPr_SpecialN.c, ftPr_SpecialN.h, and types.h hunks
notes: The loaded raw diff is truncated in the PR packet, but targeted loaded excerpts plus grep-visible later hunks cover the changed files and most relevant patterns.
The bot report provides match/improvement/regression evidence, but the slice does not include objdiff command lines, build logs, or per-function rationale for stack tactics.
No human review comments are present, so reviewer intent or acceptance reasoning cannot be inferred.
The author explicitly stated that some functions were not 100% matches; partial improvements should not be recorded as fully matched despite high percentages.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pair fighter struct layout recovery with special-move decomp
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2305, recovering ftPurin motion vars and attributes in types.h enabled many ftPr_SpecialN functions to use typed fields and contributed to 27 new 100% matches in the Purin SpecialN unit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep match/regression reports bidirectional
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2305 shows why match reports should record both wins and regressions: the same bot report listed 27 new Purin matches and 3 broken Kirby matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:bot report new matches and broken matches

source_update_candidates.title: ftPr_SpecialN.c is Purin/Jigglypuff SpecialN rollout implementation area
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2305 changed src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.c as the primary implementation file for Purin SpecialN/SpecialAirN rollout behavior, with supporting type and init-data declarations in ftPurin/types.h and ftPr_Init.{c,h}.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoping fact for future curator/search tasks; should be verified against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explicit notation for intentional partial-match PRs
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR intentionally lands close-but-not-100% functions, record which functions are partial and preserve the bot/objdiff evidence rather than letting high-percent improvements be searched as full matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md:author says some changes felt close enough
text_corpus.jsonl:bot report lists 7 unmatched-item improvements below 100%
source_update_candidates.reason: This is already aligned with verification-ledger standards, but PR #2305 is a concrete example that may be useful as source-corpus guidance if curator wants to add examples.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat stack padding and dummy byte arrays as evidence-required match tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2305 uses PAD_STACK and dummy u8 local arrays in ftPr_SpecialN.c while the bot report confirms broad progress. Curator may want this as an example under the existing matching-tactics standard: keep these tactics narrow and tie them to per-function match evidence.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl:bot report ftPr_SpecialN matches and partial improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already cover this; this PR can serve as an example candidate, not a new accepted rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all functions in ftPr_SpecialN.c were 100% matched; the author note and bot report explicitly identify some partial improvements.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; the slice has no review comments or reviews.
Do not promote PAD_STACK or dummy u8 arrays as normal style; evidence only shows they were present in this PR and associated with match progress.
Do not claim the Kirby broken matches were accepted tradeoffs by reviewers; the slice only shows the bot report, not review discussion.
